links.targetTitle: Each reactor IChannel exposes three mailboxes: inbox, outbox, and deadLetter.

global.title: Sync state is persisted in three PGlite tables owned by SyncManager: remotes, cursors, and dead letters
global.content:
The reactor's SyncManager persists sync state in PGlite (local Postgres-in-WASM) rather than ad-hoc plugin storage. Three tables cover the surface:

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `sync_remotes` | Registered remotes with channel_type, push/pull state, failure counts |
| `sync_cursors` | Per-remote ordinal-based cursor tracking for inbox and outbox |
| `sync_dead_letters` | Failed operations with error details for retry |

Because these are durable reactor tables, channel implementations like SwarmChannel don't need their own persistence layer — cursors survive page reloads and failed ops remain queryable.

The plugin layer's own `plugin/state.ts` is kept minimal by comparison: it holds only UI-related state (Bee URL, UI cache fields, and drive mapping for the settings panel). This lets the channel evolve without leaking implementation state into the plugin.
global.description: sync_remotes holds registered channels with failure counts; sync_cursors tracks per-remote ordinal cursors for inbox and outbox; sync_dead_letters persists failed ops with error detail for retry.
